visual_intent: Hierarchical diagram showing the hypothesis structure. Main hypothesis H1 at the top: 'Geometric filtering improves text classification vs SMOTE'. Four sub-hypotheses branching below: H1a (Filtering: distance-based filtering improves sample quality, linked to 3675 configs), H1b (Soft Weighting: continuous weights match or beat binary filtering, linked to 1890 configs), H1c (NER Generalization: filters generalize to named entity recognition, linked to 63 configs), H1d (Classifier Effect: linear classifiers benefit more, linked to 3675 configs). Each hypothesis shows its acceptance criteria: positive magnitude, p<0.05 Bonferroni, win rate >50%.
planner_desc:
**Global Layout and Aesthetics**
The figure is a top-down hierarchical tree diagram set against a pure white background. The layout is clean, symmetrical, and utilizes a modern academic aesthetic with rounded rectangular nodes, subtle drop shadows for depth, and crisp sans-serif typography (e.g., Arial or Helvetica). Connections between nodes are drawn using 2px thick, dark gray (#4B5563) orthogonal (stepped) lines terminating in downward-pointing arrowheads. 

**Main Node (Top Level)**
*   **Position & Shape:** Centered at the top of the canvas is a large, prominent rounded rectangle.
*   **Color & Style:** The box features a deep professional blue background (#1E3A8A) with crisp white text. 
*   **Text Content:** 
    *   **Header:** "H1: Main Hypothesis" in bold, slightly larger font.
    *   **Body:** "Geometric filtering improves text classification vs SMOTE" centrally aligned.
*   **Lower Pane:** The bottom third of the box is separated by a fine, semi-transparent white horizontal line. Inside this pane, the text reads: "Acceptance Criteria: Positive Δ | p < 0.05 (Bonferroni) | Win Rate > 50%" set in a slightly smaller, italicized font.

**Sub-Hypothesis Nodes (Second Level)**
Branching downwards from the main H1 node are four evenly spaced sub-nodes arranged in a single horizontal row. Each sub-node is a rounded rectangle, slightly narrower than the main node, featuring a distinct light pastel background with dark text for clear differentiation. Each sub-node is structurally divided into three parts: a bold header, a descriptive body, and a standardized lower pane for criteria, accompanied by a pill-shaped badge for configurations.

*   **Node 1: H1a (Filtering)**
    *   **Color:** Soft pastel teal background (#E0F2FE) with dark teal text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1a: Filtering" (Bold).
        *   **Body:** "Distance-based filtering improves sample quality."
    *   **Badge:** A small, vibrant blue pill-shaped badge overlapping the top-right corner of the node, containing white text: "3,675 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** A distinct light gray footer section at the bottom of the node with text: "Criteria: Δ > 0, p < 0.05, Win > 50%".

*   **Node 2: H1b (Soft Weighting)**
    *   **Color:** Soft pastel green background (#DCFCE7) with dark green text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1b: Soft Weighting" (Bold).
        *   **Body:** "Continuous weights match or beat binary filtering."
    *   **Badge:** A vibrant green pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "1,890 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Light gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: Δ > 0, p < 0.05, Win > 50%".

*   **Node 3: H1c (NER Generalization)**
    *   **Color:** Soft pastel purple background (#F3E8FF) with dark purple text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1c: NER Generalization" (Bold).
        *   **Body:** "Filters generalize to named entity recognition."
    *   **Badge:** A vibrant purple pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "63 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Light gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: Δ > 0, p < 0.05, Win > 50%".

*   **Node 4: H1d (Classifier Effect)**
    *   **Color:** Soft pastel orange background (#FFEDD5) with dark brown/orange text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1d: Classifier Effect" (Bold).
        *   **Body:** "Linear classifiers benefit more from augmentation."
    *   **Badge:** A vibrant orange pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "3,675 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Light gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: Δ > 0, p < 0.05, Win > 50%".

**Connections and Flow**
A single solid vertical line descends from the bottom center of the H1 node, which then splits horizontally into a crossbar. From this horizontal crossbar, four vertical lines drop down, each terminating with a solid dark gray arrowhead pointing exactly to the top-center edge of nodes H1a, H1b, H1c, and H1d, respectively. The routing of the lines is strictly orthogonal (90-degree angles) to maintain an organized, systematic appearance.
stylist_desc:
**Global Layout and Aesthetics**
The figure is a top-down hierarchical tree diagram set against a pure white background, embodying the "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic of a modern NeurIPS paper. The layout is clean, symmetrical, and utilizes "Softened Geometry" with 8px rounded rectangular nodes and subtle drop shadows for depth. A strict typographical hierarchy is applied: all structural text and labels use crisp, modern sans-serif fonts (e.g., Helvetica or Roboto), while all mathematical variables and operators (e.g., $\Delta$, $p$) are set in an italicized serif font (LaTeX default). Connections between nodes are drawn using 2px thick, dark gray (#4B5563) orthogonal (stepped) lines terminating in distinct, downward-pointing arrowheads.

**Main Node (Top Level)**
*   **Position & Shape:** Centered at the top of the canvas is a prominent rounded rectangle (8px corner radius).
*   **Color & Style:** Functioning as the structural anchor, the box features a deep, professional blue background (#1E3A8A) with crisp white text.
*   **Text Content:** 
    *   **Header:** "H1: Main Hypothesis" in bold sans-serif, slightly larger font.
    *   **Body:** "Geometric filtering improves text classification vs SMOTE" centrally aligned in regular sans-serif.
*   **Lower Pane:** The bottom third of the box is separated by a fine, semi-transparent white horizontal line. Inside this pane, the text reads: "Acceptance Criteria: Positive $\Delta$ | $p < 0.05$ (Bonferroni) | Win Rate > 50%". Variables ($\Delta$, $p$) are rendered in serif math font, set in a slightly smaller, elegant size.

**Sub-Hypothesis Nodes (Second Level)**
Branching downwards from the main H1 node are four evenly spaced sub-nodes arranged in a single horizontal row. Each sub-node is an 8px rounded rectangle, slightly narrower than the main node, featuring a distinct, highly desaturated pastel background (10-15% opacity look) with color-matched dark text for clear but soft differentiation. Each sub-node is structurally divided into three parts: a bold header, a descriptive body, and a standardized lower pane for criteria, accompanied by a pill-shaped badge for configurations.

*   **Node 1: H1a (Filtering)**
    *   **Color:** Pale Ice Blue background (#E0F2FE) with dark technical blue text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1a: Filtering" (Bold Sans-Serif).
        *   **Body:** "Distance-based filtering improves sample quality."
    *   **Badge:** A medium-saturation blue pill-shaped badge overlapping the top-right corner of the node, containing white text: "3,675 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** A distinct pale gray footer section separated by a subtle dashed line, with text: "Criteria: $\Delta > 0$, $p < 0.05$, Win > 50%" (math variables in serif).

*   **Node 2: H1b (Soft Weighting)**
    *   **Color:** Mint/Sage Green background (#DCFCE7) with dark green text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1b: Soft Weighting" (Bold Sans-Serif).
        *   **Body:** "Continuous weights match or beat binary filtering."
    *   **Badge:** A medium-saturation green pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "1,890 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Pale gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: $\Delta > 0$, $p < 0.05$, Win > 50%".

*   **Node 3: H1c (NER Generalization)**
    *   **Color:** Pale Lavender background (#F3E8FF) with dark purple text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1c: NER Generalization" (Bold Sans-Serif).
        *   **Body:** "Filters generalize to named entity recognition."
    *   **Badge:** A medium-saturation purple pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "63 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Pale gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: $\Delta > 0$, $p < 0.05$, Win > 50%".

*   **Node 4: H1d (Classifier Effect)**
    *   **Color:** Warm Beige/Orange background (#FFEDD5) with dark brown text.
    *   **Content:** 
        *   **Header:** "H1d: Classifier Effect" (Bold Sans-Serif).
        *   **Body:** "Linear classifiers benefit more from augmentation."
    *   **Badge:** A medium-saturation orange pill-shaped badge at the top-right containing white text: "3,675 Configs".
    *   **Lower Pane:** Pale gray footer identical to Node 1: "Criteria: $\Delta > 0$, $p < 0.05$, Win > 50%".

**Connections and Flow**
A single solid vertical line descends from the bottom center of the H1 node, which then splits horizontally into a crossbar. From this horizontal crossbar, four vertical lines drop down, each terminating with a solid dark gray arrowhead pointing exactly to the top-center edge of nodes H1a, H1b, H1c, and H1d, respectively. The routing of the lines relies strictly on orthogonal (90-degree) elbows to maintain the precise, systematic appearance standard in top-tier architectural diagrams.
